name: Vegetable Chili with Millet
recipeIngredient: 1 large Bell pepper, green, cored, seeded and 1/4 inch diced
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the beans in a large saucepan. Add boiling water to cover and let sit for 1 hour.
2. Drain the beans, then cover with fresh water and bring to boil over high heat.
3. Reduce heat to medium, cook partially covered until tender but not soft – about 50 minutes to an hour.
4. Drain and set aside.
5. Heat oil in a large saucepan over medium-high heat.
6. When hot but not smoking, add onions, garlic, bell pepper, jalapeno. Reduce heat to medium and cook, stirring frequently, until onions turn translucent – about 9 minutes.
7. Add reserved beans, 2 cups water, tomatoes, tomato sauce, spices, and oregano. Mix well and bring to boil.
8. Reduce heat to medium-low, cook partially covered for 1-1/2 hours.
9. Add millet and remaining 2 cups water, stir, and continue cooking until millet and beans are cooked through but not mushy, the flavors have mellowed, and the chili is rich and aromatic – at least 2 hours. Stir occasionally, ensuring the mixture doesn't stick to the bottom of the pan.
10. Salt to taste.
11. To serve, ladle the chili into warmed bowls and top with yogurt, scallions, and oranges or pass them separately.
